# criblio_project (Resource)

Project Resource

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "criblio_project" "my_project" {
  consumers = {
    # ...
  }
  description = "Default project configuration"
  destinations = [
    "Output:main",
  ]
  group_id = "Cribl"
  id       = "project-default"
  subscriptions = [
    "sub-default",
  ]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tools/codegen -->
## Schema

### Required

- `destinations` (List of String)
- `group_id` (String) Worker group ID.
- `id` (String)
- `subscriptions` (List of String)

### Optional

- `consumers` (Attributes Map) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--consumers))
- `description` (String)

<a id="nestedatt--consumers"></a>
### Nested Schema for `consumers`

Optional:

- `connections` (Attributes List) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--consumers--connections))
- `disabled` (Boolean) It should be removed as a consumer is present or absent.
- `type` (String)

<a id="nestedatt--consumers--connections"></a>
### Nested Schema for `consumers.connections`

Required:

- `output` (String)

Optional:

- `pipeline` (String)

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = criblio_project.my_criblio_project
  id = jsonencode({
    group_id = "Cribl"
    id       = "project-abc123"
  })
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import criblio_project.my_criblio_project '{"group_id": "Cribl", "id": "project-abc123"}'
```
